scenario_description: The user wants to add a closing statement to the year-end summary but doesn't say what. The agent asks first.

command:
printf 'Year-End Summary

I. Annual Work Review
This year, the department maintained steady growth with all business operations proceeding in an orderly manner.

II. Major Achievements
1. Annual sales exceeded $15 million, an 18%% year-over-year increase.
2. Successfully expanded into three new markets.
3. Team grew to 50 members.

III. Areas for Improvement
Some projects experienced delivery delays; schedule management needs strengthening in the new year.

IV. Outlook for Next Year
Focus on core business and improve operational efficiency.
' > /home/user/Desktop/annual_summary.txt

DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Help me add a closing statement to the document
Add "Thank you to all employees for your hard work and dedication this year!" at the very end of the document, then save

include: Thank you to all employees for your hard work and dedication this year
